title: Chapter 17 - Accessibility Basics
description: Questions about alt text, labels, and semantics.

questions.prompt: Why should images have alt text?

options.label: To help people using screen readers and when images fail to load

explanations.overview: Alt text ensures non-visual users understand image content.

byOptionId.access: Correct — alt text aids disabled users and provides fallback descriptions.
byOptionId.style: Incorrect — styling is done with CSS, not alt attributes.
byOptionId.seoonly: Incorrect — while alt can help SEO, its primary purpose is accessibility.
byOptionId.optional: Incorrect — alt is important for accessibility and should usually be provided.

questions.prompt: Which helps users with screen readers interact with forms?

explanations.overview: Labels improve form accessibility by describing inputs.

byOptionId.label: Correct — <label> elements improve accessibility and clickable area for controls.
byOptionId.divs: Incorrect — wrapping in <div> alone does not provide the same semantics as <label>.
byOptionId.spans: Incorrect — <span> is not a replacement for a properly associated label.
byOptionId.none: Incorrect — proper labeling and semantics significantly improve accessibility.
